meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn how to leverage SaaS starter kits with Antigravity and Claude Code to launch professional-grade apps fast.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, bypass boilerplate code by using a production-ready SaaS starter kit. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for intelligent CLI-based development, you can ship a functional, scalable SaaS in days, proving your engineering capabilities to potential employers through concrete technical outcomes.
content.intro:
The job market doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about what you can ship. You’re staring at a blank repository, paralyzed by the gap between theory and the complex, production-hardened systems that companies actually build. This is the 'no portfolio' void—the silent killer of entry-level engineering careers.

But here is the secret: senior engineers rarely build from scratch. We use leverage. By adopting a professional SaaS starter kit, you aren't just writing code; you are orchestrating an ecosystem. You’ll stop debugging configuration files and start building business logic that proves you can operate at an industry-standard level.

steps.title: Initialize the Foundation
steps.description: Select a robust stack and use Antigravity to scaffold the core architecture. This ensures you have authentication, database ORM, and billing integrated from the first commit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 15 project with Supabase auth, Stripe integration, and Tailwind CSS. Include a clean folder structure for feature-based modularity.

steps.title: Define Domain Models
steps.description: Use Claude Code to generate your database schema based on your app's unique value proposition, ensuring type safety across the entire stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema for a subscription-based SaaS, including User, Subscription, and Project entities with proper relations.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate prisma schema based on SaaS requirements.prisma"

steps.title: Implement Core Logic
steps.description: Build your primary feature set. Focus on high-impact functionality that demonstrates your ability to handle state and API interactions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build an API route for handling user project creation, including server-side validation and error handling patterns.

steps.title: Integrate Production Monitoring
steps.description: A portfolio project without observability is just homework. Add logging and performance tracking to show you think like a DevOps engineer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement Sentry integration for client-side and server-side error tracking in a Next.js environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "setup sentry monitoring for production environment"

steps.title: Optimize for Deployment
steps.description: Prepare your app for the real world. Automate your CI/CD pipeline so every push is a production-ready deployment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, type checks, and deploys to Vercel upon push to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate github actions pipeline for auto-deployment"

pro_tips: Always commit your 'Why' in the README; recruiters want to know the engineering trade-offs you made, not just the features.
Use mock data factories to seed your database; it makes your demo feel alive the moment a recruiter logs in.
Focus on one complex feature—like real-time collaboration or a complex dashboard—rather than five simple CRUD pages.
Keep your git history clean; squash your commits so your project timeline looks like a professional development sprint.
Add a 'Technology Decisions' document to your repo to articulate why you chose specific libraries over others.
content.next_steps: Once your project is live, write a 'Post-Mortem' blog post detailing the hardest bug you squashed during development to demonstrate your problem-solving depth.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Use these tools to build the product that lands you your first role. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
